blocks.content: After dining with the royalty of Iron and Arachne, Gis returned to his chambers to receive reports.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: The angels have left Delta?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Yes. Satiel ignored even her contractual obligations to Jive and led her forces out.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Hmm, that prideful angel?
For demons, contracts were negotiable, but for angels, they held a binding force far stronger than what humans could comprehend.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Yes. Also...

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: It seems Shirone followed shortly after. Additionally, seismic activity was detected near the Jive border.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: They must have found Ikael or whatever she's called. Let it slide. If we nitpick everything, there'll be no one left to work with.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: There's concern it might become a topic of international discussion.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Then spin it like it wasn't a Holy War Assembly matter, you idiot. Do I have to teach you everything?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Understood. Also...
The aide pulled out a newspaper.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: What?
Midway through buttoning his fresh shirt, Gis snatched the paper.
The headline read: "Exposing the Corruption of the Holy War Assembly" by a journalist named Maekel.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Him again.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: A top graduate of the Royal Political Academy. He's been writing critical articles about the monarchy for years.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: This is a nuisance. Were the Arachne people always this sloppy? That's why you don't deal with bargain-bin fools.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: What should we do?

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Try bribing him. If that doesn't work, offer more. Didn't they say his son is sick?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Yes, congenital heart disease. The boy's been hospitalized for two years now.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: How's his condition?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Improving steadily, from what I've heard.
Gis paused, his hand hovering over the last button, and turned to his aide.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Hey, aide.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Yes, Your Majesty.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Do you have children?
The question seemed redundant since Gis already knew, leaving the aide momentarily confused.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Yes. My eldest daughter is about to start school, and my second is four this year.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Imagine if one of them were sick like that. How would you feel?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: I'd feel terrible. It would probably feel like my heart was being ripped apart.
Gis's eyes glinted sharply as he swung his arm, slapping the aide hard across the face.
The aide stumbled and fell, and Gis followed with a kick to his stomach.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: If you know that, why are you handling this so poorly?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: I'm sorry!

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: If a child's sick, the parent's heart is torn apart, isn't it? Then what should you do, huh?
Gis unleashed a flurry of kicks to the aide's ribs as he cowered on the floor.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: You make it worse! You push them further! Buy off the doctor, choke the kid's lifeline, make them gasp for every breath! Isn't that what you should do?
Snatching the nameplate from Havitz's desk, Gis struck the aide across the head.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Is your brain full of crap? Can't you think? Get out there and turn everything upside down!

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Please forgive me!
Even as blood ran from his face, the aide couldn't bring himself to agree.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: How can anyone do that? Even as a politician who's committed countless corrupt acts, there's a line I can't cross.
Seeing the aide remain steadfast, Gis lowered the nameplate, panting heavily.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: This guy, huh?
It seemed the aide intended to endure a beating and leave it at that.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Get up.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Y-yes!
Dazed but obedient, the aide shot to his feet, brushing off his clothes.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Do you plan to do politics like this? Spend your life cleaning up after me? Is that all you're capable of?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: No, Your Majesty.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: I'm someone who donates to foundations. Do you know how many patients I've saved? Do you think your petty conscience is helping this society?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: The state exists to handle these situations. You're smart, aren't you? Why are you projecting your life onto someone else's kid?
Placing a hand on the aide's shoulder, Gis leaned in.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Or do I need to make your kid sick so you'll understand what reality is?
The aide's eyes widened in shock.
A sob escaped, and he fell to his knees, clutching Gis's leg.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Please forgive me! I just can't do it! Your Majesty, please, have mercy on me!
His hands trembled uncontrollably in fear.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: You're pathetic.
As much as he wanted to beat the man to death, the aide's utility outweighed his temper.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Fine, get up. You're useless.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Thank you, Your Majesty! I'll devote myself entirely to your service!
Patting the aide's back, Gis's tone softened.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: I wasn't being literal, you know. You don't have to dirty your own hands.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Excuse me?
Seeing the bloodied aide blink in confusion, Gis's irritation flared again.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: If you can't do it, hire someone who can! What do you think gangsters are for? Find some fool and pay him to get it done. Do you understand? You know how to do this.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Y-yes, Your Majesty.
Gis motioned toward the door with his chin.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Why are you still standing here? Go get it done! Do I have to spoon-feed you too?

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: I'll handle it immediately!
As the aide bolted from the room, Gis changed into fresh clothes.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Honestly, you can't find good help these days. How am I supposed to get anything done if my team doesn't pull their weight? Unbelievable.
Adjusting his tie, Gis glanced in the mirror.
Behind him stood Havitz, reflected lazily yawning.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Are there no competent people around? Other kingdoms are full of sharp folks. Do I have to do everything myself?

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: How dull.
Anywhere he went in the human world, whatever mind he read, Havitz was sick to death of it all.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: At least I got a clue.
Even as Havitz opened the door and stepped out, Gis failed to notice his presence.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: By the way...

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: Who tipped them off? A trap? No, Arachne wouldn't have the guts.

blocks.speaker: Gis
blocks.content: The thought of having to restrain his indulgences for a while made him mutter: "Damn it..."
Γùå Γùå Γùå
Thormia Sector
Having received news that Shirone had left Delta, Lupist convened the leadership.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Albino and Phony were among those gathered as Lupist tossed a newspaper onto the table.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: This is today's daily from Jive Kingdom. They didn't include it in the morning edition, likely to avoid censorship.
Plu opened the paper.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I saw this before I came. It's still just speculation, but Jive is hosting the Holy War Assembly. This might shake Gis's position.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: It won't be a major blow. Someone like Gis, with his level of power and wealth, can ruin a person with ease.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: Still, it might limit his actions. He'll probably hold off on attending banquets. Have we identified the informant?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Not yet. It's unlikely to be a trap. Arachne's use of honey traps isn't so sloppy.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Then why did someone tip them off?

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: It's human nature. Something probably happened that night. Once someone loses control, they'll do anything.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: So, what's your point? Why did you call us here?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: I want to make use of this incident. Personally, I think it's a clue.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: Politically bury it, then. That's more peaceful than assassination. But how?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: It seems Arachne's honey trap has failed. But Gis is like a beast. Not being able to attend the banquet will leave him seething. Perhaps we can exploit that opening.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: So now you're saying Thormia should try a honey trap too?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Exactly. Gis will be extremely cautious. But if someone approaches who can break through that wariness? Then things could get interesting.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: And who's this person?
When Lupist and Albino silently turned to her, Plu pointed at herself.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Me?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Say no if you don't want to.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: No, I agree it's a good plan. But I've never trained in honey traps. Plus, I'm not particularly attractive.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Don't underestimate yourself. You're more than capable. We factored in your situational judgment and adaptability when deciding.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: What's more, the reason this will work is that Gis is in a state of high anxiety. Safety-first thinking. A discreet liaison with the Thormia Magic Association's secretary-general would be an irresistible bait.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What if he decides to stay cautious?

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: Testosterone is powerful. Historical precedent proves that, beyond just scientific theory. Of course, it depends on how much trust you can earn from him, but the odds are in your favor.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Hmm...
As Plu ran simulations in her mind, Albino turned to Lupist.

blocks.speaker: Albino
blocks.content: ...So, the time is now?

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Yes. Shirone would oppose it and might even break the alliance over it. But for now, we still need Yahweh. This plan has to be carried out in secret.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: What's your answer?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Meanwhile, Havitz wandered the streets of Jive.
So many people.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Hosting the Holy War Assembly seemed to bring a sense of pride, with events held daily.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Finding someone here isn't hard.
The decadent, gambling-addicted daughter of Gis stood out, even among the crowd, thanks to her noisy thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: At the casino, she was shouting.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Yes! Bet! 200,000 gold!
Holding a drugged glass of wine, it was clear even without Havitz's divine frequency that she wasn't in her right mind.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Gis's daughter.
If he destroyed the child of the man who had no qualms about ruining others' children, how would Gis react?

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: I want to hear that sound.
It wasn't an obsession, but it would be some entertainment in this dull Holy War Assembly.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: This is why I need history searches.
Simply killing or tormenting someone wouldn't yield the kind of ultimate sound he sought.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Approaching the casino table with his sword in hand, Havitz went unnoticed.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Vanishing.
If he stabbed Rebecca, the nations of the Holy War Assembly would blame him.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: But this isn't part of the Assembly, is it?
Paying Gis back with his own logic was part of Havitz's twisted enjoyment.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Alright, alright! This time, 300,000! I'll clean up big!
Standing behind Rebecca, Havitz glanced around briefly before raising his sword.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Should I start with an arm?
But at that moment, something caught his eye.
A seven-year-old child stood there.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: Are you Gustav Havitz, the scourge of the age?
Havitz stared blankly, glancing left and right before pointing at himself.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: You can see me?

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: I'm here to kill you.
Even with divine frequency, the child's mind revealed no murky malice.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: Wena... Wizard?
Her name pulsed clearly in his mind.
